data.body:
ROOT CAUSE PROVEN + FIXED (rebar-debug protocol, worktree fix-review-sidecar-race).

MECHANISM (runtime-confirmed): _prune_queue_events (enrich_drain.py) ran a raw UNLOCKED whole-index git rm + git commit, outside the unified store write lock (I5) and with no pathspec. In the [agents] drain child it races locked append_event writes two ways: (1) sweep-and-strand — its whole-index commit sweeps a concurrent writer's staged SIGNATURE/REVIEW_RESULT blob into the prune commit; the writer's own commit then fails 'nothing to commit' and the failure path unlinks the file from the worktree though it is in HEAD; (2) 'cannot lock ref HEAD: is at X but expected Y'. Both swallowed by sidecar.emit (->sidecar_emitted=False) and attest.sign_plan_review (->unsigned). Isolated-store experiment (remote OFF, so branch-divergence excluded): 33/360 concurrent writes swept-and-stranded on buggy code; 0 with the fix. Explains why [dev]-only concurrency did NOT reproduce (no drain => no unlocked committer).

FIX: route the deletion through the canonical locked, pathspec-scoped event_append.delete_events — the exact pattern already applied to the twin prunes plan_review/sidecar.py::prune and overlap/digest_sidecar.py (bug malevolent-emigratory-umbrette). _prune_queue_events was the un-hardened twin. One-function change.

RED->GREEN: new held-out regression tests/interfaces/store/test_enrich_drain_prune_concurrency.py — concurrent locked writers vs the real prune committer must lose zero. RED on origin/main (22/250 lost), GREEN with the fix, mutation-checked (reverting the fix returns it to RED). make lint + make typecheck green; make test running. Fix-safety: restoration (I5), no new dep (delete_events exists), no test weakened.

SIBLING (Step-7 sweep): purge_bridge.py has the same unlocked whole-index commit on the tracker — filed as grassgreen-clotty-duck (discovered_from), lower exposure (manual operator command), separate fix.
